diagrams.description: This mind map organizes the field of Sociological Theory. It branches from classical theorists and their core ideas (Marx’s conflict, Durkheim’s social facts, Weber’s rationalization), through major contemporary paradigms (functionalism, conflict, symbolic interactionism, feminism, critical race, postmodernism), to key concepts (social structure, agency, power, ideology) and persistent debates (structure vs agency, micro vs macro). It illustrates the interconnected theoretical toolkit used to analyze society.

      Consensus vs Conflict
